steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The sentence opens with a participial phrase (“Woven from recycled yarn and hand tufted...”) describing something woven and tufted. After the blank comes an adjective phrase (“so lush and tactilely inviting...”). The blank must contain a subject noun and linking verb.

steps.title: Apply the dangling modifier rule
steps.content: A participial phrase at the start of a sentence must immediately precede the noun it modifies, which becomes the subject. Choice A supplies “the topological tapestries are,” correctly making the woven object the subject. The other three either make the artist the subject—creating a dangling modifier (B, D)—or fragment the sentence (C). That matches choice A — A is correct.
